title: prunus cerasus subsp. austera
intro: Prunus cerasus subsp. austera, also known as the wild sour cherry, is a subspecies of the cherry tree native to Eastern Europe and Western Asia. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Prunus cerasus subsp. austera include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting digestive health. In herbal preparations, the fruit, leaves, and bark are used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, and the fruit is also used to make jams and preserves.
